rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CYRIL E. SMITH
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Terminating Settlement Agreement And Addendum And Entry Of Stipulated Agreement.
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/26/20. (No earlier date available.) Courtesy copies are endorsed filed copies. The current value of the trust, stated on the first page of the amended petition, must be a typo. The unfiled copy of the amended petition received by the Examiner appears to be incomplete: no exhibits are attached (no copy of the order establishing the testamentary trust, no copy of the existing settlement agreement). Douglas Smith, one of the two successor trustees appointed on 5/27/11 to serve along with the individual trustees, Caroline Schuyler and David Winton, does not appear to have joined in this petition or the agreement. THIS [AMENDED] PETITION SHOULD HAVE BEEN FILED UNDER A NEW TRUST CASE NUMBER, as this case is still for the decedent's estate.

rulings.Case Title: RE SOCORRO S. ARTATES -TRUST 1992
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition Concerning Internal Affairs Of The Trust
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/02/2020 for failure to provide the required courtesy copy of the petition and other filed documents. File stamped single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court no later than 11am on 10/13/2020. (LRSF 2.7, 14.6). The Reponse to Petition Concenring Interal Affairs of Trust was not a file stamped copy and has been recycled. File stamped single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court no later than 11am on 10/13/2020. (LRSF 2.7, 14.6).

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF THEA ARCHILLETTI
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Counsel and petitioner must both appear. Issues may include (but not limited to): Conservator settled conservatee's personal injury claim and caused trust to receive settlement proceeds without court approval; and Trustee made advance fee payments without court approval and failed to reimburse trust. ***Until further notice, the Court strongly encourages remote appearances by video or telephone. Counsel and parties may appear by video or audio-only telephone through Zoom. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall. Review the Court's 6th Amended General Order which took effect on 07/21/2020 for more information. If counsel or any party appears in person, he or she should anticipate delays at the entrance to the courthouse and inside the courthouse. Masks must be worn at all times while in the courthouse and social distancing protocols will be strictly enforced, including inside the courtroom. Anyone making an in-person appearance must first familiarize themselves with the Court's current pandemic-related requirements and protocols by reviewing the Court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF OLGA HARRIS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: hearing. appearance required. remote appearance highly encouraged. Please review the 6th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Court to determine how to proceed. 1/7/2020 Conservator Timesheets 1.9 hours - Client visit to monitor/assess. 1/18/20 Investigator visit. 2/27/2020 Conservator Timesheets 2.4 hours - Visit re: care conference and management of care. 3/20/2020 Investigator Report Filed: "The only thing in her bedside bureau was one pair of socks. There were very few pieces of clothing in the closet." Staff later confirmed that Harris needs clothing and her Case Manager was informed. Ms. Gold stated she recently moved Ms. Harris' winter clothing into storage and assured the investigator she will provide Ms. Harris an adequate supply of seasonally-appropriate clothing Mr. Vargas says it is sometimes difficult to reach the conservator. 3/25/2020 Conservator Timesheets Review/Research re: clothing purchases, client needs and budget 3/27/2020 Conservator Timesheets Phone call with Mauu, administrator re: purchasing clothing 4/2/2020 Conservator Timesheets Phone call to Hector, Hayward Convalescent re: update on client status confirmed facility received recently purchased items

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ARUNKUMAR VED TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Modification Of Trust Terms
rulings.Rulings: hearing. appearance required. remote appearance highly encouraged. Please review the 6th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. No Examiner Notes sent. 1) Court to determine whether to appoint guardian ad litem for the minor contingent beneficiaries. 2) Petitioner is requesting modification of an irrevocable trust to remove the Trustee Requirements, specifically with respect to the requirement that the Trustee (i) be a corporate trustee, (ii) have a capital surplus and undivided profits of not less than $25,000,000, and (3) have actively administered personal trusts for a term of not less than 25 consecutive years and replacing such qualifications with the following requirements: (a) a successor corporate Trustee legally required to serve as such Trustee having a capital surplus and undivided profits of not less than Twenty-Five Million Dollars ($25,000,000.00), including the capital surplus and undivided profits of the affiliates and subsidiaries of the corporate trustee, and (b) having actively administered personal trusts for a term of not less than ten (10) consecutive years, including the consecutive years of any affiliate and subsidiary of the corporate trustee.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF STEPHEN HENRY MCCOY
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/19/2020 (no earlier date available). The following issues remain unaddressed: 1) There was no Attachment 8, even though items 8 & 9 of the petition mention a one-page attachment. 2) A courtesy copy of the supplement (filed 08/19/2020) has not been received by the assigned examiner. 3) A declaration describing efforts to locate Donald McCoy and Christopher McCoy must be filed. See CRC 7.52.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ANGELA G. CHAU (CHEUNG)
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. Status of filing I&A set for 1/20/21. Status of closing estate set for 3/21/22.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ALLAN J. CRICHTON
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/26/20. (No earlier date available.) An amended pleading or an amendment to a pleading requires the same notice of hearing (including publication) as the pleading it amends. [CRC 7.53(a).] A document signed by an attorney-in-fact must be accompanied by a copy of the power of attorney. A courtesy copy of the proof of publication of notice of the petition, as amended, and a courtesy copy of the filed power of attorney executed by Richard Labate, must be received by the assigned Examiner by 10/16/20.
